## User Feedback Analysis — 2026-06-01 (elizaOS)

### Data Notes (Scope & Representativeness)
- **Primary sources in this snapshot:** Discord (2026-05-29 to 2026-05-31) and recent GitHub activity summaries (issues/PR themes referenced in weekly/monthly summaries).
- **Observed “explicit questions/help requests” on Discord:** ~6 distinct asks across 3 days (plugin location, contribution path, project status/token concerns, token migration deadline, partnership contact, unspecified “coders” issue).
- **Bias:** Feedback skews toward **Web3/DeFi builders**, **plugin contributors**, and **community ops/moderators**; comparatively little from pure Web2 or first-time “hello world” users.

---

## 1) Pain Point Categorization (Top Recurring Friction Areas)

### 1) Documentation — “Where did X go?” after repo/codebase reorg (High frequency, Medium severity)
**Examples from feedback**
- Users asked where **`plugin-solana`** went after it was moved into the main repo as **`plugin-wallet`** under `chains/solana` (Discord, 2026-05-30).
- Follow-on confusion: how to contribute new plugins (Discord, 2026-05-30).

**Who it affects most**
- Plugin developers and integrators trying to follow older docs, blog posts, or README links.

**Why it’s painful**
- Breaks onboarding momentum (“I can’t find the plugin I need”), increases repeated support load in Discord.

---

### 2) Community / Communication — Project direction & “is elizaOS abandoned?” perceptions (Medium frequency, High severity)
**Examples from feedback**
- Concern that team focus shifting to **waifu.fun** implies elizaOS is “dead/abandoned,” plus **token-sale optics** from team-associated addresses (Discord, 2026-05-30).

**Who it affects most**
- Token holders, long-term builders, and prospective partners evaluating whether to invest effort.

**Why it’s painful**
- Trust + clarity issues can reduce contributions, integrations, and partnerships even if engineering velocity is strong.

---

### 3) Technical Functionality + Documentation — Token migration UX (Low frequency, High severity for impacted users)
**Examples from feedback**
- User requested **manual migration** for pre-snapshot holdings after missing the **Feb 4 deadline**; response: migration closed, no manual path (Discord, 2026-05-29).

**Who it affects most**
- Late or less-engaged community members; anyone who missed announcements.

**Why it’s painful**
- High financial impact and reputational risk; tends to trigger repeated disputes/questions.

---

### 4) Community / Process — “Unspecified issue” routing in Discord (Medium frequency, Medium severity)
**Examples from feedback**
- A moderator asked a core dev to “investigate an issue,” but the issue wasn’t captured in-thread and no resolution was documented (Discord #coders, 2026-05-31).

**Who it affects most**
- Contributors trying to help; anyone searching later for resolution.

**Why it’s painful**
- Creates dead-end threads and duplicated debugging; knowledge isn’t retained.

---

### 5) Integration — Emerging MCP-based integrations lack a clear “golden path” (Low frequency now, Medium-to-High severity as adoption grows)
**Examples from feedback**
- Arsenal Quant’s **Decision Engine** integrates via **MCP** and uses **L402 Lightning** for payments (Discord, 2026-05-31). This is a strong signal of interest in agent monetization + external tool/oracle integrations.

**Who it affects most**
- New ecosystem builders attempting paid tools, decision oracles, and DeFi agent backends.

**Why it’s painful**
- Without reference implementations, builders may implement insecure patterns or abandon integration.

---

### 6) Community — Partnership/contact ambiguity (Low frequency, Medium severity)
**Examples from feedback**
- SKALE Labs representative asked for partnership contact info (Discord, 2026-05-29).

**Who it affects most**
- Bizdev/partners and maintainers who must route inbound requests manually.

**Why it’s painful**
- Missed opportunities; slow response times; inconsistent messaging.

---

### 7) UX/UI (Light signal) — Ongoing UI surface/test stability work suggests user-facing friction (Low frequency in Discord, Medium severity implied by GitHub focus)
**Examples from provided GitHub summaries**
- Multiple PRs referenced UI surfaces and stability (e.g., UI testing, “agentic view surfaces,” profile splitting, workspace connector error handling in recent PR activity summaries).

**Who it affects most**
- Dashboard/desktop users; teams deploying agents who need predictable control surfaces.

**Why it’s painful**
- UI instability can block adoption even when core runtime is improving.

---

## 2) Usage Pattern Analysis (Actual vs Intended Usage)

### Observed actual usage patterns
1) **DeFi/DeFAI agent integrations are a primary community driver**
   - Arsenal Decision Engine (MCP + L402) indicates builders are using elizaOS as an **agent host/orchestrator** in monetized DeFi decision workflows.

2) **Plugin-centric development is the dominant extension model**
   - Questions focus on where plugins live now, how to contribute plugins, and building new chain/DeFi plugins (Kamino integration).

3) **Community relies on Discord for operational support and “source of truth”**
   - Migration questions, repo reorg questions, and status concerns are asked in Discord rather than routed to docs/issues.

### Emerging / unexpected applications
- **Paid decision oracles + Lightning paywalls (L402) as first-class agent tools.**
  This suggests an “agent economy” direction where external tools charge per call and agents need standardized payment/auth flows.

### Feature requests that align with these patterns (implied)
- A **canonical MCP integration guide** with security and deployment best practices.
- A **plugin discovery/registry UX** (searchable index; “what replaced plugin-solana?” mapping).
- Better **contributor workflow clarity** for plugin PRs (scaffolding templates + examples).

---

## 3) Implementation Opportunities (Concrete Solutions per Major Pain Point)

### Pain Point A: Repo reorg confusion (plugin-solana → plugin-wallet under `chains/solana`)
**Solutions (prioritized)**
1) **Add a “Moved/Renamed Modules Map” doc + redirect links (High impact, Low effort)**
   - Create `docs/migrations/plugin-renames.md` and update README links.
   - Add a small table: `plugin-solana → chains/solana/plugin-wallet`.

2) **Introduce deprecation stubs or README pointers in old locations (High impact, Medium effort)**
   - If old repos/paths still exist historically, add a final commit/README that points to the new path.

3) **CLI helper: `eliza doctor` / `eliza locate plugin-solana` (Medium impact, Medium effort)**
   - Similar to how frameworks provide migration hints; this reduces Discord support burden.

**Comparable patterns**
- Many ecosystems keep “migration guides” per release (e.g., Next.js upgrade guides; Kubernetes deprecated API notices) with explicit rename maps and timelines.

---

### Pain Point B: Project status/token optics and trust gap
**Solutions (prioritized)**
1) **Publish a short, recurring “Roadmap + Resourcing” update (High impact, Low effort)**
   - Clarify: what’s maintained, what’s experimental, and how waifu.fun relates (or doesn’t) to elizaOS.
   - Include a “What shipped this month” linking to weekly/monthly summaries.

2) **Create a single “Official Statement: Token + Treasury + Team Addresses” page (High impact, Medium effort)**
   - Not necessarily exhaustive, but sets expectations and reduces rumor cycles.
   - Add a “where to ask token questions” policy to reduce scattered debate.

3) **Add a “Maintainer on-call” rotation for community Q&A (Medium impact, Medium effort)**
   - One 30–60 minute slot weekly; publish notes afterwards.

**Comparable patterns**
- Larger OSS projects reduce fear/uncertainty via lightweight governance/roadmap posts (e.g., Rust “This Week in Rust”; Vue/React RFC processes).

---

### Pain Point C: Token migration closure (no manual path)
**Solutions (prioritized)**
1) **Add a “Migration Status Checker” page with blunt outcomes (High impact, Low effort)**
   - If closed: show “Closed” + rationale + what (if anything) users can still do.

2) **Pre-write a support macro + bot command `!migration` (High impact, Low effort)**
   - Ensures consistent answers, reduces emotional escalation.

3) **Consider a narrowly scoped exception policy (High impact, High effort/risk)**
   - If legally/technically feasible: define criteria, timeline, and process.
   - If not feasible: explicitly document why (security, contract finality, compliance).

**Comparable patterns**
- Token migrations in other communities often use hard deadlines, but successful ones provide **self-serve verification tools** and **clear finality messaging** pinned across channels.

---

### Pain Point D: Discord issue triage is lossy (“investigate an issue” with no artifact)
**Solutions (prioritized)**
1) **Require a linkable artifact for technical asks (High impact, Low effort)**
   - Rule of thumb: “No issue without: repro steps / logs / link / screenshot.”
   - Add a Discord bot prompt when “issue” is mentioned in #coders.

2) **Auto-create GitHub issues from Discord threads (High impact, Medium effort)**
   - Use a simple form that posts to GitHub with context and assigns labels.
   - Ensures resolution is documented and searchable.

3) **Adopt a lightweight triage template in Discord (Medium impact, Low effort)**
   - `Environment:`, `Version:`, `Steps:`, `Expected:`, `Actual:`, `Logs:`.

**Comparable patterns**
- Many OSS Discords route bug reports into GitHub via bots/forms to prevent “lost debugging” (common in Rust, Prisma, Supabase communities).

---

### Pain Point E: MCP + paid tool integrations lack a standard reference path
**Solutions (prioritized)**
1) **Ship an “MCP Integration Cookbook” (High impact, Medium effort)**
   - Minimal working example: tool server, auth, observability, retries, sandboxing.
   - Include a “DeFi oracle” example referencing patterns like Arsenal’s use case.

2) **Security guidance: MCP spawning + destructive actions hardening (High impact, Medium effort)**
   - Consolidate recent security hardening work into a practical guide: safe defaults, allowlists, secrets handling.

3) **Reference implementation for paid calls (L402) (Medium impact, Medium effort)**
   - Even a community-maintained example repo would accelerate adoption and reduce insecure custom implementations.

**Comparable patterns**
- Stripe/Shopify-style “integration guides” succeed because they provide a canonical flow + sample code + failure modes.

---

### Pain Point F: Partnership contact ambiguity
**Solutions (prioritized)**
1) **Add `partners@` contact + web form + SLA expectation (High impact, Low effort)**
2) **Create a “Partnerships” GitHub Discussion category (Medium impact, Low effort)**
3) **Publish a one-pager: supported integrations, what elizaOS is/not, how to co-market (Medium impact, Medium effort)**

---

## 4) Communication Gaps (Expectations vs Reality)

### Gap 1: “Active development” exists, but visibility is uneven
- Reality: significant engineering activity is ongoing (numerous PRs, stability/security work).
- User expectation: if focus shifted to another product, they infer abandonment.
**Fix:** Monthly roadmap + “what shipped” digest pinned in Discord and mirrored on GitHub Discussions.

### Gap 2: Migration deadline finality not internalized by latecomers
- Recurring pattern: users discover migrations late and expect manual remedies.
**Fix:** Pin a migration status post, add `!migration` bot macro, and include migration state in onboarding/start-here.

### Gap 3: Repo movement breaks mental models
- Users rely on older names (`plugin-solana`) and can’t reconcile new structure.
**Fix:** rename map, redirects, and “breaking changes” notes in release announcements.

### Gap 4: “How do I contribute a plugin?” is still asked
- Even when the answer is “open a PR,” users need the *operational* steps (scaffold, tests, style).
**Fix:** plugin authoring guide + template repo + checklist.

---

## 5) Community Engagement Insights

### Power users / key contributors observed
- **odilitime**: frequent helper/moderator; addresses repo structure questions, project status concerns, and triage; also merges major security hardening work (per GitHub summary).
- **stan0473**: core dev/mod; receives investigations; needs better intake artifacts to act efficiently.
- **n4vnt**: plugin builder (Kamino integration interest); benefits from clearer plugin contribution scaffolding.
- **faouzi_122**: ecosystem builder introducing MCP + L402 integration; candidate for a case study / reference integration.

**Power-user needs**
- Faster, more structured triage intake (reduce ambiguous “please look” pings).
- Clear plugin architecture guidance post-reorg.
- Official blessing of integration patterns (MCP, payments, security).

### Common newcomer questions indicating onboarding friction
- “Where is X plugin now?”
- “Is the project abandoned?”
- “Can I still migrate?”
- “Who do I contact for partnerships?”

### Converting passive users into contributors
- Create a **“Good First Plugin Tasks”** list (docs fixes, rename-map PRs, example integrations).
- Recognize builders who publish integration writeups (e.g., spotlight Arsenal Quant MCP integration).
- Host a monthly “plugin demo day” in Discord to channel energy into documented artifacts.

---

## 6) Feedback Collection Improvements

### Current channel effectiveness
- **Discord:** fast, but lossy; technical issues often lack reproducible artifacts and resolutions aren’t archived.
- **GitHub:** good for durable work tracking, but many users never make it there (they ask in Discord instead).

### Improvements for more structured, actionable feedback
1) **Add Discord → GitHub issue intake**
   - Simple form-based bot posting issues with required fields.
2) **Standardize “Support vs Bug vs Feature” routing**
   - Pin guidance: where to ask what; require version info for bugs.
3) **Monthly feedback pulse (5-question survey)**
   - Ask: top blocker, plugin usage, deployment mode, docs gaps, desired integrations.

### Underrepresented segments (missing feedback)
- **Non-Web3 / Web2 automation users** (little evidence in this snapshot).
- **First-time local installers** who may struggle with environment setup but don’t post publicly.
- **Enterprise/security evaluators** (despite SOC2 work, little direct feedback captured here).

---

## Prioritized High-Impact Actions (Next 2–4 Weeks)

1) **Publish and pin a “Moved/Renamed Components Map” (plugin-solana → plugin-wallet, etc.)**  
   *Impact:* immediate reduction in repeated questions; unblocks plugin builders. *Difficulty:* low.

2) **Create a single “Project Status & Roadmap (monthly)” post + Discord pin**  
   Include: relationship to waifu.fun, what’s actively maintained, and links to weekly/monthly shipped items.  
   *Impact:* reduces abandonment rumors; improves partner confidence. *Difficulty:* low.

3) **Add `!migration` bot macro + Migration Status page with finality messaging**  
   *Impact:* reduces high-emotion support threads; sets clear expectations. *Difficulty:* low.

4) **Implement a lightweight triage workflow for #coders (required repro/logs + GitHub issue creation option)**  
   *Impact:* fewer dead-end investigations; better searchable resolutions. *Difficulty:* medium.

5) **Start an “MCP Integration Cookbook” with one end-to-end example (tool server + auth + observability)**  
   *Impact:* accelerates emerging DeFAI/agent-economy integrations; improves security posture by standardizing patterns. *Difficulty:* medium.

# elizaOS Discord - 2026-05-31

## Summary

### Arsenal Quant Decision Engine

faouzi_122 introduced Arsenal Quant and the Arsenal Decision Engine project, a high-performance decision oracle designed for DeFi agents. The system integrates with elizaOS through MCP (Model Context Protocol) and implements the L402 Lightning protocol for payment processing. The project targets the DeFAI (Decentralized Finance AI) space, providing decision-making capabilities for autonomous DeFi agents.

### Community Activity

Multiple community members exchanged morning greetings across channels. dondada0420 inquired about project progress. odilitime mentioned another user to investigate an unspecified issue in the coders channel, which stan0473 acknowledged.

## FAQ

**Q: What is Arsenal Quant?**
A: Arsenal Quant is a high-performance decision oracle designed specifically for DeFi agents, featuring the Arsenal Decision Engine that provides decision-making capabilities for autonomous DeFi agents in the DeFAI space.

**Q: How does Arsenal Decision Engine integrate with elizaOS?**
A: The Arsenal Decision Engine integrates with elizaOS via MCP (Model Context Protocol).

**Q: What payment protocol does Arsenal Decision Engine use?**
A: Arsenal Decision Engine implements the L402 Lightning protocol for payment processing.

## Help Interactions

odilitime requested stan0473 investigate an unspecified issue. stan0473 acknowledged the request. No resolution was documented in the chat segment.

## Action Items

### Technical

- Investigate unspecified issue (mentioned by odilitime, assigned to stan0473)

# elizaOS Discord - 2026-05-30

## Summary

### Codebase Reorganization

The plugin-solana component has been relocated and integrated into the main eliza repository. The plugin is now available as plugin-wallet under the chains/solana subdirectory, representing a significant structural change to the elizaOS codebase architecture.

### Plugin Development and Contributions

Active development is underway for a plugin-kamino integration for elizaOS. The contribution process for new plugins follows standard pull request procedures to the official repository. Previous Kamino integration work exists that includes read functionality for version 1.x, which may serve as a reference for ongoing development efforts.

### Project Status and Direction

The elizaOS project remains active despite team focus shifting to waifu.fun. Ongoing projects continue to involve the elizaOS token, and the project is not abandoned. Community concerns were raised about team commitment to the ElizaOS token following observed token sales from team-associated addresses.

## FAQ

**Q: Where is plugin-solana located now?**
A: Plugin-solana has been moved and integrated into the main eliza repository as plugin-wallet, specifically under the chains/solana subdirectory.

**Q: How can I contribute a new plugin to elizaOS?**
A: New plugins can be contributed through pull requests to the official repository.

**Q: Is elizaOS still being actively developed?**
A: Yes, elizaOS is neither dead nor abandoned. While team focus has shifted to waifu.fun, there are ongoing projects involving the elizaOS token.

**Q: Is there existing code for Kamino integration?**
A: Yes, there is code from a previous Kamino integration attempt that includes read functionality for version 1.x, which can be shared as a reference.

## Help Interactions

**Helper:** odilitime
**Helpee:** n4vnt
**Resolution:** Clarified that plugin-solana has been moved to the main eliza repository as plugin-wallet under chains/solana subdirectory. Confirmed the contribution process for the plugin-kamino development and offered to share existing Kamino integration code with read functionality for version 1.x.

**Helper:** odilitime
**Helpee:** btcordinals888
**Resolution:** Addressed concerns about project status by clarifying that elizaOS is not dead or abandoned, explaining that while team focus shifted to waifu.fun, ongoing projects continue with the elizaOS token.

## Action Items

### Technical

- Complete development of plugin-kamino for elizaOS (mentioned by n4vnt)
- Review and potentially utilize existing Kamino integration code with read functionality for version 1.x (mentioned by odilitime)

### Features

- Submit pull request for plugin-kamino contribution to official repository (mentioned by n4vnt)

# elizaOS Discord - 2026-05-29

## Summary

### Partnership and Business Development

SKALE Labs representative chistormze reached out seeking partnership contact information for collaboration opportunities with the elizaOS project.

### Token Migration

A user inquired about manual migration options for pre-snapshot holdings from August 2025 after missing the February 4th deadline. The moderator confirmed that the migration period has officially closed and manual migrations are no longer available.

### Trading Analysis

Detailed 0DTE options trading analysis was shared covering put and call wall dynamics in the 730-755 price range, including specific trading strategies based on dealer hedging behavior and structural support levels.

### NFT Activities

Brief mention of an NFT drop without additional context or details provided.

## FAQ

**Q: Can I still manually migrate my pre-snapshot holdings from August 2025 if I missed the February 4th portal deadline?**
A: No, manual migration is no longer available as the migration period has closed.

**Q: What are the key trading levels for 0DTE options strategies?**
A: Long bias above 741 targeting 745-747, defensive positioning around 734-735, and avoidance of the 735-740 no-trade zone due to dealer hedging dynamics.

## Help Interactions

**Helper:** odilitime
**Helpee:** doc022597
**Issue:** Request for manual migration assistance for pre-snapshot holdings from August 2025 after missing the February 4th deadline
**Resolution:** Clarified that manual migration is no longer available as the migration period has closed

## Action Items

### Technical

None identified.

### Features

None identified.

### Documentation

None identified.

ElizaOS Community Discussion and Builder Activity - May 31, 2026
---
Community members exchanged greetings throughout the day in the general discussion channel. A notable introduction came from Faouzi, founder of Arsenal Quant, who is building the Arsenal Decision Engine, described as a high-performance decision oracle for DeFi agents. The project integrates with elizaOS via MCP and uses L402 Lightning for payments. Faouzi expressed enthusiasm about connecting with other builders in the DeFAI space.

In the coders channel, Odilitime flagged an issue for Stan to investigate, and Stan acknowledged it and indicated he would look into it. Separately, a community member posted a paid opportunity seeking a reliable partner for simple operational tasks and business relationship management. The role requires a native English speaker with clear communication skills, the ability to participate in client meetings, and a preference for stable income. The position is listed as remote and is open only to residents of the US, Canada, and UAE, with no technical experience or degree required.

## ElizaOS Community Discussion and Builder Activity - May 31, 2026

## Community Introductions

- Faouzi, founder of Arsenal Quant, introduced himself and his project, the Arsenal Decision Engine
- The Arsenal Decision Engine is described as a high-performance decision oracle for DeFi agents
- The project integrates with elizaOS via MCP and uses L402 Lightning for payments
- Faouzi connected with other builders in the DeFAI space

## Builder and Coder Activity

- Odilitime flagged an issue in the coders channel for Stan to investigate
- Stan acknowledged the issue and committed to looking into it

## Community Opportunities

- A paid remote opportunity was posted seeking a partner for operational tasks and business relationship management
- The role targets native English speakers with clear communication skills
- Requirements include the ability to participate in client meetings and a preference for stable income
- The position is open to residents of the US, Canada, and UAE

# elizaOS Discord - 2026-05-31

## Summary

### Arsenal Quant Decision Engine

faouzi_122 introduced Arsenal Quant and the Arsenal Decision Engine project, a high-performance decision oracle designed for DeFi agents. The system integrates with elizaOS through MCP (Model Context Protocol) and implements the L402 Lightning protocol for payment processing. The project targets the DeFAI (Decentralized Finance AI) space, providing decision-making capabilities for autonomous DeFi agents.

### Community Activity

Multiple community members exchanged morning greetings across channels. dondada0420 inquired about project progress. odilitime mentioned another user to investigate an unspecified issue in the coders channel, which stan0473 acknowledged.

## FAQ

**Q: What is Arsenal Quant?**
A: Arsenal Quant is a high-performance decision oracle designed specifically for DeFi agents, featuring the Arsenal Decision Engine that provides decision-making capabilities for autonomous DeFi agents in the DeFAI space.

**Q: How does Arsenal Decision Engine integrate with elizaOS?**
A: The Arsenal Decision Engine integrates with elizaOS via MCP (Model Context Protocol).

**Q: What payment protocol does Arsenal Decision Engine use?**
A: Arsenal Decision Engine implements the L402 Lightning protocol for payment processing.

## Help Interactions

odilitime requested stan0473 investigate an unspecified issue. stan0473 acknowledged the request. No resolution was documented in the chat segment.

## Action Items

### Technical

- Investigate unspecified issue (mentioned by odilitime, assigned to stan0473)

# Overall Project Weekly Summary (May 24 - 30, 2026)

## Executive Summary
This week, the ElizaOS project focused on maturing our infrastructure and strengthening the reliability of our AI agent framework. By enhancing security standards, improving agent decision-making, and expanding our ecosystem through community-led plugins, we have significantly increased the framework's stability for both Web2 and Web3 environments.

### Key Strategic Initiatives & Outcomes

**Strengthening Security and Reliability**
_Goal: We aimed to ensure our framework meets professional standards for security and operates consistently without unexpected errors._
- We implemented industry-standard security controls (SOC2) and improved our release process to ensure that every update is verified and secure ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We replaced unstable health checks with a more reliable heartbeat system to keep our cloud services running smoothly ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

**Refining Agent Intelligence**
_Goal: We wanted to make our AI agents more predictable and better at following instructions without getting stuck in loops._
- We updated the "planner" logic to prevent agents from repeating tasks or narrating their own internal processes, ensuring they focus on delivering raw, useful data ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We introduced a new tool for evaluating prompts, which helps agents make better decisions during complex tasks ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

**Expanding Ecosystem Capabilities**
_Goal: We are building a modular framework where community members can easily add new features to support diverse use cases._
- We successfully integrated community-built plugins for Solana security, treasury management, and AI compliance, proving the strength of our collaborative model ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We added support for decentralized identity standards and new AI inference providers, broadening the framework's utility in the Web3 space ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

## Repository Spotlights

### elizaos/eliza
- **Improved Agent Logic:** Fixed issues with task duplication and meta-narration to ensure cleaner agent output ([#7969](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7969), [#7955](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7955)).
- **Enhanced Security:** Added SOC2 compliance controls and automated security scanning to protect the framework ([#8014](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8014)).
- **Infrastructure Upgrades:** Enabled multi-architecture support (amd64/arm64/riscv64) and improved release integrity with new verification standards ([#7971](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7971), [#7906](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7906)).
- **Ecosystem Growth:** Integrated community plugins including `plugin-prflght` (Solana firewall), `plugin-gblin` (Base treasury management), and AgentAudit ([#7938](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7938), [#7939](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7939), [#7974](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7974)).
- **Identity & Inference:** Added support for ERC-8004 identity registries and the NEAR AI inference provider ([#7937](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7937), [#7883](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7883)).
- **Stability Fixes:** Resolved critical API errors and replaced HTTP health probes with Redis heartbeats to improve service uptime ([#7992](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7992), [#7899](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7899)).

2026-05-01.md
---
# Overall Project Monthly Summary (May 2026)

## Executive Summary
May 2026 was a month of foundational hardening for ElizaOS, focused on stabilizing our core framework and ensuring seamless compatibility across diverse computing environments. By resolving critical infrastructure bottlenecks and standardizing how our plugins interact with the core system, we have significantly improved the reliability and security of our AI agent deployments.

### Key Strategic Initiatives & Outcomes

**Strengthening Core Reliability and Cross-Platform Stability**
*Goal: To ensure our agents run consistently and securely on any server or operating system.*
- We hardened the agent runtime for headless environments, preventing system crashes on Linux and Windows servers ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We implemented a new, secure way to manage sensitive information across different platforms, making it easier to integrate with our settings interface ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We resolved complex system-level errors that previously hindered reliable agent performance in headless deployments ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow)).

**Modernizing Our Build Architecture**
*Goal: To align our plugin ecosystem with modern web standards for better performance and easier maintenance.*
- We transitioned multiple plugins ([plugin-ollama](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-ollama), [plugin-openrouter](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter), [plugin-pdf](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-pdf), and [plugin-openai](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openai)) to a modern, modular build structure, eliminating runtime conflicts with the core framework.

**Ensuring Secure and Reliable Integrations**
*Goal: To maintain high security and service continuity for our decentralized exchange and communication tools.*
- We migrated our Jupiter integration to the official API endpoint and implemented mandatory security key authentication to keep our decentralized trading features compliant and reliable ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter)).
- We refined configuration management for Telegram and Discord plugins to ensure that agent settings are applied correctly and consistently during runtime ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram), [elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord)).

### Cross-Repository Coordination
- **Shared Framework Alignment**: A major project-wide effort was undertaken to align all plugins with the ESM-first architecture of the core framework. By removing legacy code formats across multiple repositories, we ensured that the entire ElizaOS ecosystem remains compatible, modular, and free of runtime errors.
- **Unified Runtime Fixes**: Coordination between the core framework and the n8n-workflow plugin allowed us to resolve deep-seated issues regarding authentication and message handling, ensuring that user settings propagate correctly from the dashboard to the agent runtime.

## Repository Spotlights

### elizaos/eliza
- Hardened the runtime for headless servers by bypassing unavailable system secret services ([#7230](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7230)).
- Introduced `@elizaos/vault` to enable secure, cross-platform secrets management ([#7197](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7197)).
- Fixed critical authentication regressions, including SIWE failures and bot token bridging ([#7288](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7288), [#7327](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7327)).
- Improved user interaction by adding clarification workflows for n8n and better context integration for Discord ([#7316](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7316), [#7315](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7315)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow
- Formalized how agents request missing information from users by implementing `ClarificationRequest` structures ([#27](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/27)).
- Improved prompt routing reliability by switching to ID-based directives ([#28](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/28)).
- Resolved critical runtime crashes and message loss issues that affected Telegram bot polling ([#7244](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/7244), [#7245](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/7245)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter
- Successfully migrated to the official `api.jup.ag` endpoint to ensure service continuity ([#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).
- Implemented mandatory API key authentication to meet modern security standards for swap requests ([#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord
- Addressed configuration inconsistencies where voice modules bypassed standard auto-reply settings ([#49](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/issues/49), [#50](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/50)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram
- Fixed a configuration issue to ensure Telegram setup tokens are correctly applied during runtime execution ([#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/29)).
